Protein names: Cytidylyl-2-hydroxyethylphosphonate methyltransferase (EC 2.1.1.308) (MeCbl-dependent radical SAM C-methyltransferase Fom3)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the radical SAM superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=cytidine 5'-{[hydroxy(2-hydroxyethyl)phosphonoyl]phosphate} + AH2 + 2 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = cytidine 5'-({hydroxy[(S)-2-hydroxypropyl]phosphonoyl}phosphate) + 5'-deoxyadenosine + L-methionine + A + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 2 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59072, ChEBI:CHEBI:13193, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:17319, ChEBI:CHEBI:17499, ChEBI:CHEBI:57844, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:142876, ChEBI:CHEBI:142877; EC=2.1.1.308; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:28678474}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59073; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:28678474};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in fosfomycin biosynthesis (PubMed:17220970, PubMed:24370735, PubMed:28678474). Catalyzes the C-methylation of cytidylyl-2-hydroxyethylphosphonate (HEP-CMP) to form cytidylyl-2-hydroxypropylphosphonate (HPP-CMP). The C-methylation is not stereoselective and the ratio of (S)- to (R)-HPP-CMP is almost equal in vitro (PubMed:28678474). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17220970, ECO:0000269|PubMed:24370735, ECO:0000269|PubMed:28678474}.
